headline: Fund manager: Follow the money into AI hardware (Nvidia, SK Hynix)
detail: A credible manager rotating INTO compute hardware and OUT of software validates the thesis behind your chip-heavy book - SMH, SOXL, and names like TSM and ARM in your scanner. This isn't a surprise catalyst but it reinforces that institutional flows are still chasing the infrastructure layer of AI, not the software layer. Good tailwind for your core holdings if it continues.
rec: No specific trade triggered by this alone, but it keeps TSM on the radar - scanner shows an ACCUMULATE setup (entry 443.34-443.79, stop 442.47). Only worth considering after you've resolved SOXL and SPAL; don't add chip exposure while leveraged losers are open on margin.

headline: Nvidia offers startups compute-for-equity revenue share
detail: Nvidia is essentially buying future revenue share in AI startups by trading compute access - this deepens its moat and keeps demand for its hardware sticky even when cash-strapped customers can't pay upfront. For your holdings, this is structurally bullish for Nvidia's ecosystem and indirectly supports the semiconductor infrastructure trade. No immediate price jolt, but confirms Nvidia's dominance is being actively fortified.
rec: No action needed today - this is a medium-term positive for the sector broadly (SMH, SOXL), not a same-day trade trigger. Monitor whether SMH builds on this narrative before adding.

headline: Google loses $4.7B EU antitrust fine appeal
detail: This is a tech regulatory headline but hits Google/Alphabet specifically, not your chip or AI infrastructure names directly. It does keep the 'big tech regulatory risk' narrative alive, which can create general sector jitter in QQQ and SPY. Your exposure to both means a broad tech selloff from regulatory fear could nudge your index positions.
rec: No action - your QQQ and SPY stops (670 and 705) are well below current prices and this headline doesn't threaten those levels. Just background noise for now.

headline: Warsh comment injects life into debasement trade - for one day
detail: A fleeting move into inflation/debasement hedges (gold, real assets) driven by a Fed-adjacent comment is noise for a one-day move, but worth noting because sustained dollar debasement fears can rotate money OUT of growth tech. Your 90% tech/chip concentration is most exposed if this theme gains real traction. One day does not make a trend.
rec: No action today - one day of debasement talk doesn't move your stops or change your setups. If this theme persists over several sessions, it becomes a reason to lighten leveraged positions first, which you arguably should be doing with SOXL and SPAL anyway.
